title: Build a Strategy that Addresses Your Gnarliest Challenges
description: Explains the concept of the "crux" in strategy - identifying the central challenge that must be overcome for progress to occur - and discusses how organizations can focus their efforts on resolving such constraints.

title: Strategic Coherence for Tumultuous Times
description: Examines how organizations can maintain strategic coherence in periods of economic and technological disruption by aligning policies, actions, and resources around a clear diagnosis of the situation.
file: articles/Strategic challenges & coherence for tumultuous times.pdf

title: Getting Strategy Wrong
description: Discusses common errors in strategy formulation, particularly the tendency to confuse goals, financial targets, and aspirations with strategy. The article emphasizes the role of diagnosis and focused action in addressing difficult challenges.

title: Why Bad Strategy Is a Social Contagion
description: Examines how ineffective strategic thinking spreads through organizations and industries, and what leaders can do to resist its influence.

title: The Perils of Bad Strategy
description: Describes common patterns in ineffective strategy, including the substitution of slogans or ambitions for analysis and the avoidance of difficult choices about priorities and trade-offs.

title: The U.S. Economy Needs to Break Its Stimulus Habit
description: Opinion essay discussing macroeconomic policy and the long-term effects of repeated fiscal stimulus on economic growth and structural adjustment.

title: Setting Strategy in the New Era
description: Discussion of strategic decision-making following the financial crisis, focusing on uncertainty, capital allocation, and changing competitive conditions. Conversation with Lowell Bryan and Allen P. Webb.
file: articles/setting strategy in the new era a conversation with lowell bryan and richard rumelt.pdf

title: Management Lessons from the Financial Crisis
description: Conversation examining management implications of the global financial crisis, including corporate governance, risk management, and the role of strategic leadership. With Lowell Bryan and Allen P. Webb.
file: articles/management lessons from the financial crisis a conversation with lowell bryan and richard rumelt.pdf

title: Weighing the US Government's Response to the Crisis
description: A dialogue examining the effectiveness and long-term implications of US government interventions during the financial crisis.
file: articles/weighing the us governments response to the crisis a dialogue.pdf

title: Strategy in a Structural Break
description: Discusses how major shifts in economic or technological conditions ("structural breaks") alter competitive landscapes and require organizations to reconsider existing strategies and assumptions.

title: Strategy's Strategist: An Interview with Richard Rumelt
description: An interview discussing Rumelt's views on the nature of strategy, including the importance of diagnosing the core problem and distinguishing strategy from planning or goal setting. With Dan P. Lovallo and Lenny T. Mendonca.
file: articles/strategys strategist an interview with richard rumelt.pdf

title: The Honda Effect Revisited
co-author: Henry Mintzberg, Richard T. Pascale, Michael Goold
description: Revisits the debate about Honda's entry into the U.S. motorcycle market and the broader question of deliberate versus emergent strategy. With Henry Mintzberg, Richard Pascale, and Michael Goold.

title: Understanding Business Model Reinvention
description: Describes a means of organizing and presenting issues in business model reinvention

title: Your Business Model Reinvention Journey: Where to Begin
description: Business model reinvention begins with an assessment of your current business model and those used by competitors as well as by your industry as a whole.
file: articles/Your Business Model Reinvention Journey Where to Begin.pdf

title: The Reinvention Journey: Finding Opportunities in the Storm
description: Today, each megatrend is both accelerating and changing in character. Together, they are colliding to create an era of turbulent change. This new era poses both great risks and significant opportunities.
file: articles/The Reinvention Journey-Finding Opportunity in The Storm.pdf

title: Lessons From Past Technological Disruptions
description: What lessons can we learn from past technological disruptions? This note examines seven technological disruptions occurring in the last thirty years.

title: Your Business Model Reinvention Journey: Dealing with Inertia
description: What makes organizational change so difficult? In a word, organizations have inertia.
file: articles/Your Business Model Reinvention Journey-Dealing with Inertia.pdf

title: A Bibliography on Business Model Reinvention

title: Verizon strike: Can both sides win?
description: Verizon's management and the unions are responding to immediate issues and changes, but they are both acting myopically.
